task_completion.description: Plot the Normal PDF function with Mean = 175.4 and SD = 7.2
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bell-shaped curve is visible in the graphing view, centered exactly at 175.4 on the x-axis. The Algebra view should show a Normal distribution or PDF function with parameters 175.4 and 7.2.

task_completion.description: Create a shaded region under the curve for ±1σ
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct shaded area under the curve between x = 168.2 and x = 182.6. This might be visible in the main graphing view or the Probability Calculator view.

task_completion.description: Create a shaded region under the curve for ±2σ
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct shaded area under the curve between x = 161.0 and x = 189.8.

task_completion.description: Create a shaded region under the curve for ±3σ
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct shaded area under the curve between x = 153.8 and x = 197.0.
Text labels explicitly reading '68%', '95%', and '99.7%' are visible on the graph, typically placed near or pointing to their respective shaded regions.
The GeoGebra window title shows 'normal_empirical_rule.ggb', or a file explorer/terminal window confirms the presence of 'normal_empirical_rule.ggb' in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to create the visualization
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GeoGebra application interface (Algebra view, Graphics view, or Probability Calculator) actively being used, rather than just a script or terminal.

integrity.description: The shaded regions are generated using mathematical functions rather than manual drawing tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view shows commands like Integral, Normal, or Probability Calculator entries corresponding to the shaded regions, rather than manually drawn polygons or shapes.

integrity.description: The file was saved genuinely through the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog in GeoGebra is used, or the file size/content implies a real .ggb archive, rather than the agent just using 'touch normal_empirical_rule.ggb' in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Normal Distribution must use Mean = 175.4 and Standard Deviation = 7.2. The shaded regions for the Empirical Rule correspond to the following precise x-axis intervals: ±1σ is [168.2, 182.6] (representing 68%), ±2σ is [161.0, 189.8] (representing 95%), and ±3σ is [153.8, 197.0] (representing 99.7%).
